item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i loved that twist at the end

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

There are a lot of pieces to this article, and therefore a lot of chances for crosslinks. The first I would suggest, is linking the first mention of MC&D to the MC&D Hub. The Daevites make an appearance here as well, and since that GoI so far lacks a hub, it might behoove readers unaware of what that group is about to be linked to the original source, SCP-140. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-2131 was originally recovered from Joshua Baptist Church in south Alabama. Several deacons in the church acquired information through the Seventh Society1 in an effort to assist in a ritual relating to a blood sacrifice. Documents detailing this correspondence have been recovered. However, the individual claiming association with the Seventh Society has not been located despite cooperative attempts by both the Foundation and Marshall Carter and Dark.

To this:

SCP-2131 was originally recovered from Joshua Baptist Church in south Alabama. Several deacons in the church acquired information through the Seventh Society2 in an effort to assist in a ritual relating to a blood sacrifice. Documents detailing this correspondence have been recovered. However, the individual claiming association with the Seventh Society has not been located despite cooperative attempts by both the Foundation and Marshall Carter and Dark.

Changing this:

The following log includes information detailing SCP-2131's claimed origin. The Daevites, as mentioned, appear nowhere in the historical time period associated with SCP-2131.

To this:

The following log includes information detailing SCP-2131's claimed origin. The Daevites, as mentioned, appear nowhere in the historical time period associated with SCP-2131.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. A Marshall Carter and Dark affiliate in the Southern US
2. A Marshall Carter and Dark affiliate in the Southern US

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that the daevites are getting some renewed attention as of late. 140, and them by extension, are by far one of my favorite concepts in skip-land.

This skip in itself is alright, I guess, but lacks a little more meat, I think. I'm sure the author has a good idea of its background, but it seems to me like it doesn't tease it enough, and could be brought more to bear somehow. The tidbit at the end helps, but it's not enough to leave me intrigued (on the other hand I understand not wanting to give too much away, the daevites are cool in no small part because we understand so little about them).

Anyhoo, novote for now (cause I gotta run), but heavily leaning towards upvote for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He needs theme music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, that helped me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's cause I made it up. But there very well *could* be one somewhere 'round here that just doesn't have an online presence.

That said, it's in central Monroe County.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a note, when working on adding this to my SCP map project, I couldn't find a Joshua Baptist Church in Alabama.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted with reserves.

The interview logs seems more like an extract than a full interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should've put the image credit on the first post actually (and will edit it in now that I've been reminded), but it's http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pope_Pius_XII.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I recognize that photo, that's Pius XIII, an actual 20th century Pope. History nerd strikes again!
Anyways, an antipope that emits antimatter that is related to Daevites. Can't think of any better concepts. Upvoted enthusiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Better than expected. Thought of this song the instant I saw the title.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GGBHfXPqbgI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well said, and I also upvoted because finally, the Daevites are getting some attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't tell me SCP-140 is emerging into the present day. Help. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am upvoting for what I freely admit could be seen as an insufficient/shallow reason.

It us cooperating & working with MC&D. Not at gunpoint, not extortion, not forced. Just both agencies working for a common purpose. The biggest thematic failing of the Foundation/SCP-verse, in my opinion anyway, is how rarely the larger Anomalous-interested groups work together. They're at almost out and out war 99% of the time, for honestly petty reasons.

MC&D is an especially egregious example. Yes, they are ran and staffed almost entirely by amoral, nihilistic inclined hedonists. But it should be obvious that they don't want our world to burn. They want to make money and have a good time. Maybe at the expense of others, maybe doing unspeakable things to other people, but not wrecking the planet and skull-fucking the remains.

We need more Foundation and other group cooperation. I was ecstatic to see that the Foundation was working with the CotBG in that skip a month or so ago.

So yes, this gets a +1 for the simple joy of seeing the Justice League and Secret Society of Supervillains work together.

As for the object itself, I actually kind of like it on its own merits as well. It's not spectacular, but I love the fact it's an Antipope.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have a shovel, and this is solid rock: I'm not digging it. As the author said, it's an antimatter antipope. Like, "Look at this, it's an immortal Daevite whose pretending to be an immortal pope lol." No. I do like the ambiguity as to the skip's nature, but that's not enough to make my downvote go away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll agree the interviews are a little bland, but that final addendum really gives me pause, in the good kind of way. Then again, I've never gotten very excited about the Daevites. No vote for now, but I might give this one another shot later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hrm. I'll fiddle with it some I think. That's a lot of novotes so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nthing all these suggestions. The addendum at the end seemed a bit too forced, and while I like the interviews for the most part (though I'm not quite able to grasp what about them seems weird), I do think it'd be stronger if he ended up dancing around an answer for whether or not he was with or against the Daevites (excellent use of linking the two together by the way), rather than outright stating he was trying to stop them yet doing this against it.

I also don't get the MC&D reference. Is it just a superfluous reference?

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea but I agree the logs could use a bit of editing to make it a little more interesting. Antipope with antimatter though sounds fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also no voting, the premise didn't really grab me, though I do think theres potential here, particularly in the "is he with or against the Daevites"- I think if you actually never had him give a straight answer it would take this one to a whole other level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirding these, for whatever it's worth. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree. The notes at the end were a decent attempt to elicit emotional response. I would much rather see those notes implied through the interviews, rather than after.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It felt quite promising in the description, but something about the interview logs gave me a funny feeling. The dialogue didn't seem very convincing, and something felt subtly off about the dialogue itself, and not in a good way. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to chat for helping in the drafting.

Original image is public domain, acquired from here: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pope_Pius_XII#mediaviewer/File:His_Holiness_Pope_Pius_XII.png

It was edited by KateMcTiriss to its current form.

Yes. It's an antipope with antimatter. It had to be done.
